---
title: Ideation & Project Kickoff Workflow
description: Structured process for capturing, expanding, and prioritizing project ideas at the start of a new project.
version: 1.0
---

# Ideation & Project Kickoff Workflow

## Purpose

- To capture, structure, and prioritize all initial project ideas.
- To facilitate brainstorming and expansion of concepts with the help of an LLM.
- To create a clear, actionable foundation for project planning (MVP, epics, features).

## Steps

### 1. Capture Initial Ideas

- Add all raw ideas to `docs/ideas.md`.
- Group related ideas by theme if possible.
- Ensure each idea is clearly described.

### 2. Structure and Expand Ideas

- Use the LLM to brainstorm, expand on strong ideas, and explore new directions.
- Organize ideas into logical groups.

### 3. Tag and Prioritize Ideas

- Assign an importance tag to each idea:
  - **MVP** (Must Have)
  - **Nice to Have**
  - **Stretch Goal**
- Assign a priority score or ranking (e.g., 1–5 or High/Medium/Low) to MVP and Nice to Have ideas.
- Use the [Idea Prioritization Skill](../skills/idea-prioritization-skill.md) for detailed guidance.
- Convert ideas into a checklist or table for tracking progress, tags, and priorities.

### 4. Review and Refine

- Regularly revisit the checklist or table.
- Mark completed ideas.
- Revisit and update tags and priorities as the project evolves.

### 5. Transition to Planning

- Use the refined, prioritized ideas to:
  - Draft the MVP
  - Define epics
  - Plan features

## Checklist/Table Example

| Idea                   | Tag          | Priority | Done |
| ---------------------- | ------------ | -------- | ---- |
| User authentication    | MVP          | High     | [ ]  |
| Dark mode              | Nice to Have | Low      | [ ]  |
| AI-powered suggestions | Stretch Goal | –        | [ ]  |

## Tips

- Keep ideas concise but descriptive.
- Don’t filter ideas too early—capture everything, then refine.
- Use the LLM to challenge assumptions and suggest alternatives.
